the daily ledger of restraint not armed

on a second thought…

almost bought it · a gentle pause between wanting & paying price № you · vol. i

stores spend billions making spending frictionless. this little paper puts the friction back · a short tax of time and breath between the wanting and the paying, so the decision is made by you, and not by the spike.

set your terms below. then shop as usual: anything above your threshold gets a calm interruption · breathe with the blob, see the price in hours of your life, then walk away, park it, or buy with a clear head. everything stays on this device.

section i

the pause

when, how long, and what it asks of you.

in scaled mode this is the wait at a $100 purchase.
night guard 🌙

10pm–6am: timer +50%, shows the clock. self control runs lowest late.

close tab after walking away

lingering tabs refill carts. recommended.

the panic button skips a pause but then disables all protection for this long · make it cost something.
section ii

your units

a price tag is an abstract number your brain barely feels · that's why cards beat cash. translate every price into the currency of your own life.

monthly ÷ 160 fills the hourly figure.
pick things you buy often enough to feel.
live preview · shown on every pause
enter your pay or an anchor to see prices in your units.
right click any price on any page → convert to my units.
section iii

what passes

things you buy on purpose, not on impulse · groceries, medicine, textbooks. any product matching a term below is never interrupted.

your data

everything is yours, on this device. no servers, no accounts, no telemetry.

the column of restraint

almost bought

nothing yet · your walk aways will be recorded here, with links.
held for later

the parking lot

nothing parked. "park it" on any pause holds the urge until it matures.
made with love by the nonconsumer
saved · armed. go try a checkout.
